name: Zazaki
notes: Zazaki (Dimli / Kirmanjki), a Northwestern Iranian language of eastern Anatolia. STUB: the grapheme inventory is NOT yet specified. Zazaki is written in a Latin alphabet derived from the Kurdish Hawar alphabet, and also (historically and in some communities) in an Arabic-derived script; no authoritative letter-to-phoneme table for the Zazaki Latin alphabet could be verified, and the phoneme inventory includes pharyngealised consonants and a central-vowel contrast whose orthographic representation is not settled. Latin-script input is assumed for any future map; the Arabic-script tradition (an abjad) is out of scope.
